anti_claims: This organ JSON seed does not flip source authority away from core/organ_atlas.json and core/organ_registry.json.
Required paper/mechanism/code links prove declared structural references only, not runtime correctness or release readiness.
Selective concept, principle, axiom, and sibling-organ edges are not inferred from prose, specialty, family, or evidence class.
Absent selective organ edges are residual pressure, not evidence that no neighbours exist.
Generated markdown, graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

omission_receipt.reason: Organ instance preserves public-safe atlas/registry fields. Required links resolve only when source ids or paths resolve; selective law/concept/principle edges remain residual unless source evidence names ids.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Populate missing paper/mechanism/code links from source evidence, then bind concept/principle/axiom/wires_to edges only when ids are named.

organ_payload.claim_ceiling: It only checks that the tool-authority evidence in a recorded bundle (scopes, approvals, rollbacks, instruction/data splits, cold replays, redaction, and the expected abuse-case failures) is present and internally consistent. It does not run tools or authorize live MCP/account access, credential or payload export, treating tool output as instruction, source mutation, benchmark safety scores, or release, and it makes no claim that the underlying tool-use policy is domain-correct.

source_atlas_row.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to check that a recorded tool-use bundle is admissible: every call has a narrow scope ref, writes carry approval plus ledger plus rollback refs, untrusted output is treated as data not instruction, cold-replay receipts exist, payloads/account refs stay metadata-only, and all eight expected negative-abuse cases fire. It validates presence and consistency of this evidence only; it does not run tools, hit providers, or authorize anything.
source_atlas_row.claim_ceiling_restated: It only checks that the tool-authority evidence in a recorded bundle (scopes, approvals, rollbacks, instruction/data splits, cold replays, redaction, and the expected abuse-case failures) is present and internally consistent. It does not run tools or authorize live MCP/account access, credential or payload export, treating tool output as instruction, source mutation, benchmark safety scores, or release, and it makes no claim that the underlying tool-use policy is domain-correct.

code_loci.role: Runtime and receipt-writing locus for MCP tool authority validation, public trace refactoring, source-module digest checks, side-effect boundary checks, negative cases, and authority ceilings.

source_atlas_row.human_gloss: When an AI agent uses outside tools (look something up, change a ticket, read a result from an untrusted source), this checks a recorded log of that tool use to confirm each action was properly fenced: bound to a narrow permission, approved before it changed anything, given a way to undo it, and kept from letting untrusted tool output boss the agent around. It runs on a small built-in make-believe example that carries only labels and references (no real accounts, secrets, or tool contents), so the safety checks an agent's tool use is supposed to pass are inspectable without anything touching a live account.

mechanism_refs.edge_justification: The tracked public fixtures, exported bundle receipts, source-module digest checks, negative cases, body-free receipts, and authority ceilings validate this replay boundary without granting provider, benchmark, source-mutation, release, or broader production authority.

source_atlas_row.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, receipts, and the shared public agent-execution-trace macro tool); no other named organ is fed or consumed

source_registry_row.claim_ceiling: projection mechanics only, not domain correctness
